titleStmt.title: Letter from Malachy J. Kelly to James Brown, 23 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Malachy Kelly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is from Malachy J. Kelly (1851-1916), Chief Crown Solicitor, to James Brown asking for a statement from Dr. McIvor regarding the death and legacy of Elizabeth Hogg.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
23rd March 1916
Elizabeth Hogg decd. Dear Sir, I submitted your letter and copy affidavit of John
Duff to the Attorney General for his directions. It appears by the affidavit that Dr McIver was present
when the alleged delivery of the Deposit Receipt was made to
Mr John Duff. As the validity of a donatio mortis causa depends upon
what exactly took place a the time of the gift and the circum-
stances under which it was made, the physical and mental con-
dition of the donor, and whether she contemplated immediate
death the Attorney General would be glad to receive from Dr.
McIver a statement of what occurred on the occasion and I shall
be glad if you get one from him and send it to me as soon as convenient. Yours truly, M Kelly James Browne Esq., Solicitor, Broad Street, Magherafelt.
